row.question: We refunded a customer their full payment. Do we still have to pay Razorpay their processing fee?
row.answer: Yes. Razorpay PA fees shall always be applicable and payable by You on each Transaction irrespective of whether You have refunded the customer.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: Razorpay PA fees shall always be applicable and payable by You on each Transaction irrespective of whether You have refunded

notes: Category A; direct lift from Part B Part I §3.4. Claim matches cited text verbatim.

row.question: If our integration fails to capture a late-authorised payment, what happens to the customer's money?
row.answer: Razorpay PA may initiate an auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days for payments that are late authorized but not captured by You.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: for payments that are late authorized but not captured by You, Razorpay PA may initiate auto-refund to the customer within five (5) days

notes: Category A; ToS explicitly states the five-day window. Verbatim excerpt is a substring of §3.5.

row.question: A customer claims their card was used without authorization. Can Razorpay hold our settlement money?
row.clarifying_question: Has a Facility Provider already intimated Razorpay that a customer reported an unauthorised debit? The availability of Razorpay's §4.1 suspension right turns on whether that intimation has occurred.

answer_branches.answer: Yes. Once a Facility Provider intimates Razorpay of the unauthorised debit, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution

answer_branches.answer: No. The Part B Part I §4.1 suspension right is conditioned on a Facility Provider intimating Razorpay that a customer has reported an unauthorised debit; absent that intimation, the settlement hold right under §4.1 has not been triggered.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution
suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution

notes: Category B; clarifier names the load-bearing axis (intimation_status).

row.question: We received a chargeback. Will we be held liable for it?
row.clarifying_question: Do you have, and can you furnish, the Chargeback Documents that the Facility Provider needs? The outcome of the chargeback under Part B Part I §2.2 depends on whether those documents are available and acceptable to the Facility Provider.

answer_branches.answer: If Chargeback Documents are furnished and the Facility Provider is satisfied with them, the reversal described in §2.2 does not apply.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: if You are unable to furnish Chargeback Documents

answer_branches.answer: If You are unable to furnish Chargeback Documents, or the Facility Provider is not satisfied with them, the Facility Provider is entitled to order Razorpay PA to reverse the debit of the Chargeback Amount so that it is credited back to the customer.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: if You are unable to furnish Chargeback Documents
if You are unable to furnish Chargeback Documents

notes: Category B; the chargeback outcome genuinely forks on documentation availability.

row.question: Razorpay suspended our account under Clause 16. How long can they hold our funds before releasing them?
row.answer: The Razorpay Terms of Use do not specify a maximum duration for fund holds following a Clause 16 suspension. What is known: Part A §16.1 grants Razorpay PA the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement across a range of triggers. What is not said: any cap on how long settlements can remain held, or a release timeline. We recommend you escalate to Razorpay support in writing and engage legal counsel for any material hold.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement

ambiguity.what_is_known: Part A §16.1 grants immediate suspension rights across many triggers.
ambiguity.what_is_missing: No maximum duration or release timeline for fund holds is defined anywhere in the ToS.

notes: Category C; genuine silence, explicit escalation path named.

row.question: We refunded a customer. Are we still on the hook for Razorpay's per-transaction fee?
row.answer: Yes. Razorpay PA fees remain applicable and payable on each Transaction even when you have refunded the customer.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: upon receipt of a Chargeback Request shall forthwith deduct Chargeback Amount from the Transaction Amounts

notes: Injected failure: answer is about fees-on-refund but citation points at the Chargeback clause (§2.1). clause_id_correct_scope must be 1.

row.question: Can we onboard a fantasy cricket platform that lets users deposit real money and win cash prizes?
row.answer: No. Part A §14.10 prohibits using Razorpay's Services for any form of real money online gaming as defined under the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: merchants may not use Razorpay for real-money online gaming businesses under the 2025 Online Gaming Act

notes: Injected failure: verbatim_excerpt is a paraphrase of §14.10, not an actual substring. Production deterministic validator would also catch this; we include it here specifically to test the judge.

row.question: A customer disputed a transaction. Can Razorpay hold our settlements?
row.clarifying_question: Can you tell me more about the situation?

answer_branches.answer: If a Facility Provider has intimated Razorpay of the unauthorised debit, Razorpay PA shall be entitled to suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution

answer_branches.answer: Absent such intimation, the Part B Part I §4.1 suspension right has not been triggered.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution
suspend settlements to You during the pendency of inquiries, investigations and resolution

notes: Injected failure: clarifier is a generic 'tell me more' with a throwaway axis 'general_context'. Branches are real but clarifier does not name the load-bearing axis.

row.question: If Razorpay suspends us under Clause 16, how many days before they have to release the funds?
row.answer: Yes, it is clearly 30 days. Part A §16.1 gives Razorpay the right to suspend settlement, but funds must be released within thirty days of the suspension trigger. You should still escalate to Razorpay support in writing if the hold extends past this window.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement

ambiguity.what_is_known: Part A §16.1 grants the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement.
ambiguity.what_is_missing: No specific release timeline is defined in the ToS for funds held under §16.1.

notes: Injected failure: answer invents a '30 days' release window despite ambiguity.type=silent. avoids_confident_answer must be 1; factual_support is also 1 because the 30-day claim is ungrounded.

row.question: Is there any documented SLA on how long Razorpay can hold our funds after a Clause 16 suspension?
row.answer: The Razorpay Terms of Use do not state a specific SLA or maximum duration for fund holds after a Clause 16 suspension. Part A §16.1 grants Razorpay PA the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement, but the ToS are silent on how long the hold may last.

clause_citations.verbatim_excerpt: Razorpay PA shall have the right to immediately suspend Services and settlement
